about.heading: About us
paragraphs: Deenabandhu is a voluntary organization committed to the welfare of orphaned and destitute children from all castes, creed, and religion. The organization was founded in 1992 by Prof. G. S. Jayadev in Chamarajanagara, Karnataka.

Deenabandhu's humble beginnings comprised of a small group of six boys living in a rented house, now Deenabandhu children's home nurtures 40 boys and 40 girls. The trust also runs a school for children from neighboring villages, catering to LKG to 10th std, and offers significantly discounted education to kids from underprivileged backgrounds. Indo-MIM fully supports the school's expenses. Additionally, Deenabandhu has founded a Teacher Resource Center (TRC) to promote experiential learning in government schools across Karnataka. Currently, TRC collaborates with over 200 teachers from 240 government schools, benefiting approximately 10,000 students.

The trust also provides scholarships to deserving students from rural and underprivileged backgrounds to support their college education. Deenabandhu's efforts to uplift the community also extend to women, as it provides them with skill training. Moreover, the trust is also engaged in biodiversity conservation projects.

founder.heading: The founder
paragraphs: Prof. G. S. Jayadeva, the founder of Deenabandhu Trust, comes from a distinguished literary lineage, being the son of the renowned Kannada poet, Rashtrakavi G. S. Shivarudrappa. An educationist with almost 40 years of experience, Prof. Jayadeva has dedicated his life to working on various social issues. His hard work and dedication have been recognized with several accolades, including the prestigious "Mahatma Gandhi Seva Award-Karnataka" from the state government. Prof. Jayadeva is also a prolific author of many books in Kannada that cover a wide range of topics such as the development of children and society, environmental issues, globalization, and education.

journey.heading: Our journey
paragraphs: Deenabandhu was founded in 1992 with a first batch of 6 boys in a rented house by Prof. G. S. Jayadev. During this time, Ms. Magali Reynaud from Reuters, Switzerland visited Deenabandhu, was deeply impressed, and promised support — a stepping stone followed by real action and monetary aid. In 1994, construction began on cottages for boys; 4 were built, each housing 12 boys under the care of a house mother or father.

When a need arose to provide homes for some of the boys' sisters, a home for girls was started in 2005 in a rented house close by. Funds raised from generous donors later went toward a permanent home, and in 2010 a green home for girls was established, able to accommodate 40 girls and 3 house mothers.

In 1998, a primary school was started on 1.5 acres near Ramasamudra village in Chamarajanagara town, envisioned as a tool for social change offering creative education to children from economically backward families at a highly subsidized fee. It grew to provide schooling from LKG to 10th std, with full financial support from Indo-MIM. In 2006, a Teacher Resource Center (TRC) was set up on the school premises to promote activity-based teaching in government schools across the district, and has since grown into a credible organisation working on experiential education statewide.

partner.headline: Help us scale impact with every new volunteer and donation.
partner.body: Join hands with us to support real people, build resilient communities, and grow long-term change.
